subject: Databases
title: Adjacency List
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Adjacency List.

hook.text: Each row has a parent_id. Categories, org charts, file trees — all are hierarchies stored with adjacency lists.

buildup.text: Finding direct children is easy: WHERE parent_id = 42. Finding all descendants requires recursion.

discovery.text: Recursive CTEs in SQL traverse the tree: WITH RECURSIVE tree AS (... UNION ALL ... JOIN tree). One query, full depth.

twist.text: Adjacency lists are simple to insert and update. Moving a subtree is just one UPDATE on parent_id.

climax.text: For shallow hierarchies (under 10 levels), adjacency lists with recursive CTEs perform well.

punchline.text: Adjacency list: simple and flexible. Use recursive CTEs for tree traversal.

quiz.question: How do you query all descendants in an adjacency list model?
options: Use a recursive CTE (WITH RECURSIVE) that traverses the parent-child relationships
Query every row and filter in application code
